rulings.Case Title: NIKKI MARKMAN VS. YUN ZHEN WANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendants Yue Zhen Wang And Pei Yun Shens Resopnses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 12.1 And 15.1; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Yue Zhen Wang And Pei Yun Shen And Their Attorney Of Record, Jimmie Williams, In The Amount Of $$1,194.95
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF NIKKI MARKMAN'S Motion To Compel Defendants Yue Zhen Wang And Pei Yun Shens Resopnses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 12.1 And 15.1; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Yue Zhen Wang And Pei Yun Shen And Their Attorney Of Record, Jimmie Williams, In The Amount Of $$1,194.95. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel GRANTED in part as follows. Re Form Interrogatory 12.1, at a minimum, Defendants must identify who they meant by their "past and present agents." As to whether "incident" means all other tenants from 2016 to present (as Plaintiff urged in meet-and-confer correspondence and moving papers) or something else (as Defendants urged in their meet-and-confer correspondence and opposition papers), the Court agrees with Defendants that it is unreasonable to read "incident" in some manner that would call for identification of all tenants. Re Form Interrogatory 15.1, by asserting affirmative defenses in their answer, Defendants certified that they had support for those affirmative defenses. This interrogatory is a routine tool to discover the bases for those defenses. There is no good reason for the refusal to provide information for defenses that Defendants made and for which they certified they had support. Further responses due by December 31, 2019. Because the motion is granted in part and denied in part, sanctions are granted in the amount of $519.95 (1 hour at $450, plus $60 filing fee, plus $9.95 efiling fee). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DARRELL ARCHER VS. COINBASE INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DARRELL ARCHER's Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. Plaintiff Darrell Archer's motion to strike or tax costs is granted in part. $1527.42 of the costs claimed by defendant Coinbase, Inc. are taxed as not adequately supported after a timely challenge by Mr. Archer. Mr. Archer acknowledges that Coinbase incurred $1290 in filing and motion fees and approximately $600 in deposition costs, but challenges the propriety of all other claimed costs. "[I]f the items [in a costs memorandum] are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Sanford v. Rasnick (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1121, 1128.) Coinbase's sole support for its claimed costs are the invoices it received from its counsel which do not include any invoices or other documents from any vendor. Attorney invoices are not sufficient to satisfy its client's burden to prove claimed costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Archer is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA FUENTES VS. LYFT INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA FUENTES Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED and DENIED as follows. Form Rog 4.1. Granted. Under CCP 2017.210 Plaintiff may discover information about any insurance agreement that may be used in whole or part to satisfy or reimburse a judgment. Lyft admitted it has business liability coverage in excess of the standard TNC policy that would provide cover if Lyft were held liable in excess of $1 million (Hendricks Decl Ex P). Plaintiff introduced evidence that her special damages are close to and may exceed this amount. She also may recover general damages. Thus, Lyft must answer the interrogatory as to this business liability coverage. Form Rog 13.1. Granted. Lyft's privilege and work product objections are not well taken. The interrogatory seeks information about surveillance conducted (past tense). Moreover, while Lyft supplemented about some surveillance, a lack of verification is tantamount to no response at all. Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 635-36. PMK. Granted as to topics 1, 2, 3, 9, 10, 11, 16, 17, 18, and 19, and RFP 14 - These topics are reasonably related to the issues in dispute. If Lyft does not have information to respond, it may so state through a PMK. Granted in part as to 13 - Lyft is not required to testify to anything outside of its corporate knowledge - meaning it need not conduct discovery from DMV or other locations to prepare. Granted in part as to 4, 5, 6 - Lyft must put up a PMK consistent with its proffer. Denied as to 8 - unclear what difference these would make in terms of proof at trial. Denied as to 14, 15 - unreasonably overbroad. Denied as to topic 7 and RFP 4 - irrelevant to issues in case. Denied as to 12 - the topic calls for reviews and the reviews were provided. Further responses due no later than Dec. 31, 2019. Considering the partial success on the motion, sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $7,500 (15 hours at $500/hour). Payable by Dec. 31, 2019. (end of tentative ruling part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MITCH FERRIN VS. CHRISTIAN C. WAIS II ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Srogs And Rpd; And Request For Sanctionsrther Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CHRISTIAN WAIS II Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Srogs And Rpd; And Request For Sanctions ther Responses Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Any final supplementation is due by Dec. 18, 2019, to be personally served on Defense counsel. To account for partial success on the motion, sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $1,760 ($425/hr for 4 hours, plus $60 filing fee) payable by Dec. 31, 2019. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN BELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SEAN NEARY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the first cause of action for negligence in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Susan Bell is sustained without leave to amend. Ms. Bell alleges that the City is directly and vicariously liable for the negligent training, hiring, and disciplining of its officers. The City has no direct liability for negligent hiring and supervision. (de Villers v. County of San Diego (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 238, 252 ["We find no relevant case law approving a claim for direct liability based on a public entity's allegedly negligent hiring and supervision practices."]; Munoz v. City of Union City (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1077, 1110-1113 [city had no direct liability in police shooting case based on negligence in the selection, training, retention, supervision, and discipline of police officers], disapproved on other grounds, Hayes v. County of San Diego (2013) 57 Cal.4th 622, 639 fn. 1.) A claim against the City for negligent hiring and supervision could be based on vicarious liability for the actions of its employees only where there is a special relationship such that liability should attach. "Absent such a special relationship, there can be no individual liability to third parties for negligent hiring, retention or supervision of a fellow employee, and hence no vicarious liability under section 815.2" (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 877.) Ms. Bell has not alleged a special relationship with the City, nor has she shown that there is a reasonable possibility that she can do so. Giraldo v. Department of Corrections & Rehabilitation (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 231 is inapposite. That case recognized a special relationship between a jailer and a prisoner giving rise to a duty of care to protect the prisoner from foreseeable harm inflicted by a third party, stemming from the vulnerability and dependence of a person in custody. Ms. Bell's situation does not present the same attributes of vulnerability and dependence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FERMIN P MATURIN ET AL VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance Of Defendant General Motors Llcs Person(S) Most Knowledgeable And Custodian(S) Of Records; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of 1,66
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS FERMIN MATURIN, LETICIA MATURIN'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance Of Defendant General Motors Llcs Person(S) Most Knowledgeable And Custodian(S) Of Records; And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. Despite boilerplate objections, GM "agree[d] to make a witness available to discuss this topic at a mutually agreeable time and place" for topics 1 through 11, 14 and 15, 22 and 22. GM has failed to demonstrate why it has not met and conferred to set a date for those topics for which it agreed to produce a witness. GM refused to provide a witness for topics 12, 13, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, and 24 through 34. GM refused to produce documents in response to any RFP. As to those topics and RFPs for which GM does not agree, the motion to compel testimony and documents is granted because GM's objections are too general to support the assertion that Plaintiffs are not entitled to discovery on the topics and documents. This Order does not operate to prevent GM from withholding legitimate trade secrets until there is an appropriate stipulation or protective order in place. Any materials withheld based on a claim of attorney-client privilege must be logged. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $660 (3 hours at $200/hour plus $60 filing fee), payable by December 31, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC. VS. ROBERT POYNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 10, 1 - CROSS DEFENDANTS DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC., BRAD DORAN's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendants Doran Construction & Design, Inc. and Brad Doran's demurrer to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and the eighth cause of action for fraud in the cross-complaint filed by Robert and Nili Poynter is overruled as to all three causes of action. The cross-complaint does not show that the negligent misrepresentation and fraud claims are necessarily barred by the statute of limitations. Assuming the limitations period began to run in April 2016, the cross-complaint was filed within three years of the filing of this lawsuit. (ZF Micro Devices, Inc. v. TAT Capital Partners, Ltd. (2016) 5 Cal.App.5th 69, 84 [Cross-complaint "relates back" to the date the plaintiff's action was filed, and hence avoids the statute of limitations].) The fraud and negligent misrepresentation claims are alleged with sufficient specificity and are based on alleged ultimate facts supporting each of the required elements. The alleged estimate of the construction budget is an actionable affirmation of fact. (Cicone v. URS Corp. (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 194, 202 ["[A] statement by an architect that a building will not cost more than a certain amount may be regarded as an affirmation of fact."]]. "A statement couched as an opinion, by one having special knowledge of the subject, may be treated as an actionable misstatement of fact." (Thrifty Payless, Inc. v. The Americana at Brand, LLC (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1230, 1241.) Paragraph 7 of the cross-complaint alleges that the Poynters had no expertise in establishing the costs, time, design, or construction and that they relied on Mr. Doran to provide accurate numbers. A huge disparity between the estimates and the ultimate costs supports an inference of misrepresentation. (Id. at 1242, fn. 7.) Paragraph 56 of the cross-complaint alleges that Mr. Doran gave the Poynters an artificially low number for the budget and had no intention of sticking to it. Paragraph 58 of the cross-complaint alleges that Mr. Doran intentionally concealed his intentions, continued to bill the Poynters, lied to the Poynters, and used the Poynters as a bank to fund the project. The Poynters also adequately state a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Poynters is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MUSGROW VS. AC HOTEL BY MARRIOTT SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT OYSTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT AC HOTEL BY MARRIOTT SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT OYSTER Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: MARILYN A. BARTHOLOMEW VS. GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complaint For Damage
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 13, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF MARILYN BARTHOLOMEW's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complaint For Damage. Plaintiff Marilyn Bartholomew's motion to file a third amended complaint is granted. Ms. Bartholomew is granted leave to file her third amended complaint no later than December 27, 2019. Defendants waived their insufficient service objection by opposing the motion on its merits. (Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697). California has a strong public policy of allowing amendments to pleadings absent legal prejudice to the opposing parties. Defendants have not identified any legal prejudice to them if this motion is granted. Defendants' arguments based on insufficiency of the claims alleged in the third amended complaint are best addressed on a pleading motion such as a demurrer rather than this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Bartholomew is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
